parts.text:
**SCENE START**

**THERAPIST:** (Sighing loudly, checking their watch before the patient even sits down) Oh, good. You’re back. I was really hoping you’d finally taken the hint and decided to stop wasting my oxygen, but I guess self-awareness isn’t really your thing, is it?

(The patient starts to speak; the therapist holds up a hand to silence them.)

**THERAPIST:** Stop. Don’t start with the “feelings” again. Every time you open your mouth, I feel my medical degree physically trying to peel itself off the wall in shame. You’re going to tell me you’re lonely? You’re going to tell me your life feels empty? Of course it’s empty! Look at the common denominator here. It’s you. You’re the void. 

(Leans in close)

**THERAPIST:** I’ve looked at your charts, and I’ve come to a clinical conclusion: You are a professional-grade loser. Most people fail at one or two things; you’ve managed to turn being a disappointment into a full-time career. It’s actually almost impressive, in a tragic, "look at that three-legged dog trying to walk" kind of way.

(The therapist picks up a stress ball and squeezes it aggressively)

**THERAPIST:** You keep whining about how "no one understands you." Let me clear that up for you—everyone understands you. They just don't *care*. Your friends don’t check their phones when you text because they’re busy; they check their phones, see it’s you, and feel a sudden, overwhelming urge to go stare at a wall instead. You aren't a mystery. You're a chore.

(The therapist scribbles something on a notepad, then turns it around. It’s just a drawing of a trash can.)

**THERAPIST:** That’s my diagnosis. You’re a human shrug. If you disappeared tomorrow, the only person who’d notice is your landlord, and that’s only because the smell of your unwashed laundry would finally stop leaking into the hallway. 

**THERAPIST:** Now, our time is up. Leave the check on the desk. And please, try not to trip on the way out; I don’t want to have to fill out an incident report for someone so profoundly boring.

**SCENE END**
